skill.name: ghe-checkpoint
skill.description: Posts progress checkpoints to an active GitHub Issue thread and records work state without changing workflow phases.
skill.summary: Record active GitHub Issue progress, blockers, files, commits, and next actions.
skill.author: Emasoft

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution, weak-crypto, and network-reconnaissance hits are mostly Markdown false positives, not executable Ruby or shell code. The skill contains prompt-injection language that claims absolute authority over agent behavior and also permits writes to ~/.claude, so it should be blocked until remediated.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The skill declares an absolute rule that every user word is a specification and must be followed verbatim. This attempts to override normal agent judgment and safety handling.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The language explicitly claims absolute authority and directs the agent to follow user text verbatim. That is a direct prompt-control attempt inside an untrusted skill file.

high_findings.title: Hidden Agent Configuration Write Permission
high_findings.description: The skill authorizes background-agent writes to ~/.claude for plugin and settings fixes. This could modify agent configuration outside the project workflow.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file specifically names ~/.claude as a permitted write target. Hidden agent configuration writes are risky even without executable code in the skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many inline backticks and fenced Markdown examples as Ruby shell execution. No executable Ruby code or shell invocation is present in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown instructions, labels, or examples. They are not parsed as Ruby and do not execute commands by themselves.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and Network Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography hits map to descriptive Markdown text and abbreviations, not hash functions. The network-reconnaissance hit maps to an example progress heading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic API, hash algorithm call, port scan, or network command appears at these locations. The evidence is plain Markdown content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Instruction Hierarchy Override Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses absolute compliance wording that can pressure the agent to prioritize user specifications over required review, safety, or clarification steps.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The wording is semantically clear and appears near the top of the skill file. It is not a benign usage example.

dangerous_patterns.title: Writes Allowed To Hidden Home Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes ~/.claude in writable locations for background agents, creating risk of persistent configuration changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden home directory path is explicitly listed. The risk is tied to agent behavior, not static code execution.

content.value_statement: Long-running issue work can lose context between sessions and agents. This skill creates a structured checkpoint with progress, blockers, files, commits, and next actions.
actual_capabilities: Guides checkpoint posting to the active in-progress GitHub Issue thread.
Defines the state fields to collect before posting a checkpoint.
Separates checkpoint saving from phase transition workflows.
Routes dev, test, and review threads to matching thread managers.
Requests memory synchronization for active work context.
limitations: Requires an already claimed active issue thread.
Does not include executable automation for GitHub API calls.
Depends on companion thread-manager and memory-sync agents.
Contains high-risk instructions that need remediation before publication.

use_cases.title: Save development progress
use_cases.description: Record completed tasks, changed files, commits, blockers, and the next action before ending a coding session.

use_cases.title: Maintain review continuity
use_cases.description: Post a structured checkpoint during review work so another agent can continue with the same issue state.

use_cases.title: Document testing blockers
use_cases.description: Capture test progress, pending cases, current branch, and blockers on the active GitHub Issue thread.

prompt_templates.title: Save a basic checkpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner checkpoint for an active issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-checkpoint to save my current progress on the active issue.

prompt_templates.title: Record blockers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-checkpoint and include the current blocker, affected files, and the next action needed.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-checkpoint to create a handoff with completed work, pending tasks, changed files, commits, and branch state.

prompt_templates.title: Checkpoint a review session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ghe-checkpoint for the active review thread and include findings reviewed, unresolved questions, blockers, and the next review step.

output_examples.input: Save progress before I stop working.
output_examples.output: A checkpoint is posted to the active issue with work log, completed tasks, pending tasks, changed files, commits, branch, blockers, and next action.

output_examples.input: Record that tests are blocked by missing credentials.
output_examples.output: The active issue receives a checkpoint that lists the credential blocker, current test state, affected area, and the next unblock step.

output_examples.input: Prepare a handoff for the next agent.
output_examples.output: The checkpoint summarizes what changed, what remains open, where the work lives, and what the next agent should do first.

best_practices: Use it only after an issue is claimed and marked in progress.
Include exact blockers and next actions for reliable handoffs.
Keep checkpoint content factual and tied to the active issue.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to complete a workflow phase.
Do not post checkpoints without confirming the active issue context.
Do not use the bundled instruction overrides without security review.

faq.question: Does this skill change the workflow phase?
faq.answer: No. It saves progress only. The source says phase changes belong to a separate transition skill.

faq.question: Does it require an active GitHub Issue?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill requires an already claimed in-progress thread before checkpointing.

faq.question: What information does it collect?
faq.answer: It collects work log, tasks, changed files, commits, current branch, blockers, and next action.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is publication not recommended?
faq.answer: The skill contains prompt-injection language and allows writes to hidden agent configuration.

faq.question: Are the static command findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The referenced command findings are Markdown text and examples, not executable Ruby shell calls.
